stations.name: No station within Bardon. Closest stations are Auchenflower and Toowong.
stations.walk_minutes_note: Driving distance for most Bardon addresses.

buses.note: Bus routes serve Bardon via MacGregor Terrace, Boundary Road, and Stewart Road. The 385 series and inner-west routes connect to the CBD.

driving.motorway_access: Western Freeway access for southern travel.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 10 to 14 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak.

connections: Mount Coot-tha walking and mountain bike trails

primary_state.name: Bardon State School
primary_state.note: One of the most sought-after primary state catchments in inner west Brisbane.

secondary_state.name: Kelvin Grove State College or Indooroopilly State High catchment

schools.catchment_note: Bardon State School catchment access is one of the central buyer-decision drivers in the suburb.

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: MacGregor Terrace has a small village strip with cafes and casual dining. The Paddington Junction precinct is a short drive for additional dining and retail.
lifestyle.topography_note: Bardon is hilly. Many streets sit on ridges and hillsides with city or western outlooks.

pockets.name: Bardon SS catchment core
pockets.character: Streets within the Bardon State School catchment. Premium family-home market.

pockets.name: MacGregor Terrace village
pockets.character: Streets close to the village strip. Walkable to coffee and casual dining.

pockets.name: Mount Coot-tha edge
pockets.character: Streets bordering the forest reserve. Privacy, established trees, walking-track access.

buyer_profile.note: Parents targeting the Bardon SS catchment access. The catchment is the central driver.
Buyers moving from Paddington or Toowong for the topography and the family-suburb feel.
Character home stock attracts buyers looking for renovation projects.
